health_zone_table.basis: SitRep30 Table 1; visually reviewed from the rendered INSP PDF media 25002 (sha256 8de93e5eebbf5e97263a18de2e4aaa635e6377832372e70c5c66ad14900f53aa).

reconciliation.note: Do not allocate the Autres ZS residual to named health zones.

review_corrections: SitRep30 reports two newly touched health zones at the end of 2026-06-13: Nia-Nia in Ituri and Mabalako in Nord-Kivu.

The PDF header, facts, narrative, and map use Nia-Nia; Table 1 visually renders a Nai-Nia spelling variant. The reviewed promotion normalizes this to Nia-Nia and preserves the spelling variant in source_display_names.
The explicit Autres ZS residual remains 94 confirmed and 10 confirmed deaths; it is public but not distributed to named health zones.

contacts_total.data_gap_note: 449 new contacts were listed in eight Ituri health zones; 105 Sud-Kivu contacts completed 21-day follow-up.

challenges.finding: Reluctance to post-mortem swabbing can delay confirmation and underestimate cases.
challenges.response: Intensify community engagement and involve religious leaders.

challenges.finding: Normed CTE capacity remains insufficient despite Rwampara opening, creating potential saturation pressure.
Contact follow-up remains below target at 56.5% against a 95% target, compromising interruption of chains of transmission.
challenges.response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays.

challenges.finding: Essential medicines are insufficient in targeted HGRs, weakening care continuity.
IPC materials are insufficient in Nord-Kivu, increasing nosocomial risk.
Alert reporting remains weak across the three affected provinces.
Insufficient resources across pillars slow operations; the SitRep cites a USD 21.5M gap.

priorities.priority: Train providers on PCR RADIONE use
Brief providers on MVE surveillance and SBC in affected and high-risk zones
Brief Ituri community agents on MVE surveillance and SBC
Continue INSP Director General supervised support across technical pillars
Catch up contact follow-up in Ituri
Reinforce IPC and staff training in Nyankunde and other nosocomial zones
Deploy a daily standardized KPI dashboard by pillar
Catch up Nord-Kivu pending sample analysis
Execute Nord-Kivu actions: Goma recovered exit, security incident response, Virunga HGR IPC training, and household CREC expansion
Support Sud-Kivu with two additional vehicles, Miti-Murhesa internet kit, and communication credits for IT/ECZS

publication_context: INSP SitRep 30 reports data through 2026-06-13 and was published 2026-06-14; public INSP evidence did not expose SitRep 29 before this release.
The outbreak is reported across 3 provinces and 31 of 104 health zones; two newly touched health zones are Nia-Nia in Ituri and Mabalako in Nord-Kivu.
Ituri remains the epicenter with 717 of 782 DRC confirmed cases (91.7%).
Nord-Kivu rose to 62 confirmed cases and carries the highest provincial confirmed-case fatality ratio at 54.8%.
Sud-Kivu has reported no new confirmed case since 2026-05-26.
context: The affected Ituri area borders Uganda and South Sudan and has chronic humanitarian constraints, cross-border movement, artisanal mining, armed-group activity, and more than one million internally displaced people.
The outbreak origin remains described around Mongbwalu in Djugu territory, about 70 km from Bunia.
The outbreak was officially declared on 2026-05-15.
highlights: 72 new confirmed cases including 29 confirmed deaths were reported for 2026-06-13 in Ituri (56) and Nord-Kivu (16).
New confirmed cases were in Mongbwalu (25), Bunia (13), Rwampara (6), Nyankunde (5), Komanda (3), Nizi (2), Damas (1), Nia-Nia (1), Butembo (11), Katwa (4), and Mabalako (1).
Two new affected health zones were reported: Nia-Nia in Ituri and Mabalako in Nord-Kivu.
Five patients were declared recovered: Rwampara (3), Mongbwalu (1), and Mambasa (1).
coordination: The RDC-Ouganda cross-border meeting on the MVE-17 situation continued on 2026-06-12.
Training of MCZ and AC for the Aru pool continued.
surveillance: 507 alerts were reported and 460 investigated, producing a 90.7% investigation rate.
136 suspected cases of the day were validated, including 49 deaths.
6,275 contacts were under follow-up and 3,548 were seen, producing 56.5% follow-up coverage across the three provinces.
449 new contacts were listed in eight Ituri health zones, and 105 Sud-Kivu contacts completed 21 days of follow-up.
Two PoC were activated on the Bunia-Mahagi-Anzida axis, with 40 of 55 Ituri PoC active.
laboratory: Ituri reported 139 samples collected and analyzed, with 56 positives and 40.3% positivity.
Sud-Kivu reported 8 samples collected and analyzed, all negative.
Nord-Kivu reported 42 samples collected, 34 analyzed for Bundibugyo strain, and 16 positive results, leaving 8 samples pending from the day's batch.
infection_prevention_control: Ituri opened rings around five confirmed cases, including three in Bunia and two in Rwampara, while noting an IPC-kit gap.
Ituri reported EDS completion activity in Mongbwalu, Bunia, Komanda, Rwampara, and Bambu, plus decontamination in health facilities, households, and public buildings.
Nord-Kivu evaluated seven facilities in Beni, supported 59 facilities across Goma, Beni, Butembo, and Katwa, briefed 61 providers, and continued IPC training with WHO technical support.
Sud-Kivu received PPE/IPC supplies, assessed IPC needs at Nyakadaka, and briefed 65 providers on standard precautions and decontamination.
care_continuity: Five patients were declared recovered after negative control tests.
Creche and CTE construction monitoring continued at CME, ISTM Nyankunde, HGR Nyankunde, HGR Rwampara, and Elykia.
Across all CTEs, 470 hot meals were provided to patients and accompanying groups.
Finalization of CTE/CME-Bunia work continued, with eight tents expected to be operational by Monday 2026-06-15.
community_engagement: Nord-Kivu sensitized students, households, public-place audiences, leaders, and transport actors and reported 503 reluctance/refusal/resistance events resolved.
Sud-Kivu used radio, household visits, leader engagement, and school sensitization across Miti-Murhesa and Katana.
Ituri oriented leaders for incident prevention, conducted mass sensitization and household visits, trained relays in Mongbwalu, and supported surveillance and IPC around Rwampara areas.
The SitRep notes recurring community feedback that EDS teams sometimes arrive understaffed, prompting unprotected community assistance at burial sites.
logistics_security: The response received 164 mattresses and 140 beds for health facilities.
Twenty-eight vehicles and two ambulances were deployed across response pillars.
Motorcycles were delivered to Logo and Aungba health zones.
The security situation in and around Bunia remained calm but unpredictable.
psea: Community sensitization on complaint mechanisms continued.
redactions: Page 11 contact details are intentionally not promoted into public evidence text.

preserved_conflicts: SitRep30 headline is DRC-only (782 confirmed / 181 confirmed deaths across Ituri, Nord-Kivu, Sud-Kivu); country-scope public snapshot adds the latest reviewed Uganda component separately.
SitRep30 Table 1 includes an explicit unventilated Ituri row (94 confirmed, 10 deaths); these counts are public but not mappable to named health zones.
SitRep30 reports two newly touched health zones at the end of 2026-06-13: Nia-Nia and Mabalako; SitRep29 was not found in public INSP WordPress/API receipts before SitRep30.
Daily suspected cases (136) and suspected deaths (49) are current-cycle validated alert outputs, not cumulative suspected stock or cumulative suspected deaths.
No new named PCR-access point is promoted from SitRep30; the PCR RADIONE item is a future-priority action, not evidence of a newly operational PCR ring.
SitRep30 page 11 contains public contact details; these remain source-provenance context only and are not promoted into public evidence content.

review_notes: SitRep #030 (data cutoff 2026-06-13, published 2026-06-14) byte-captured from insp.cd media 25002; PDF sha256 8de93e5eebbf5e97263a18de2e4aaa635e6377832372e70c5c66ad14900f53aa.
DRC headline 782 confirmed / 181 confirmed deaths; +93 DRC confirmed and +42 confirmed deaths vs SitRep28 because public INSP receipts skip SitRep29.
72 new confirmed including 29 deaths are reported for 2026-06-13: Ituri 56 and Nord-Kivu 16.
Table 1 reconciles named zones 688 confirmed / 171 deaths plus explicit unventilated Ituri residual 94 confirmed / 10 deaths to 782 / 181.
Newly touched health zones are Nia-Nia (Ituri) and Mabalako (Nord-Kivu); the Nia-Nia/Nai-Nia spelling variant is normalized to the GRID3 gazetteer label.

Operational data captured: 507 alerts, 136 suspected cases of the day, 6275 contacts under follow-up, 359 patients in isolation, 181 samples analyzed, and 8 pending Nord-Kivu samples from the day batch.
Visual review kept page 11 contact details out of public evidence content; page references and PDF hash remain source provenance only.
